response.justification: Strong technical foundation with current staff role at an AI-driven organization, proven distributed systems expertise (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby), and excellent software craftsmanship. However, Python is a must-have technical skill and is entirely absent from the resume; AI/ML experience appears recent and limited in documented depth. Operational readiness and incident-response capabilities are not articulated. The candidate demonstrates the right mindset and versatility, but gaps in required skills warrant careful evaluation before proceeding.
strengths: Currently Staff Software Engineer at Acme Software working directly on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators — demonstrating baseline comfort with agentic systems as a requirement of the role
Proven distributed systems expertise at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using asynchronous I/O, caching, fibers, and high-performance databases
Strong ownership mindset with progression through multiple staff roles, founding an open-source platform, and explicit commitment to software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, Refactoring Dojos)
concerns: Python is a must-have skill but is completely absent from the resume; while demonstrating polyglot proficiency across many languages, the lack of Python is a critical gap for a staff-level distributed systems role
No documented operational ownership, monitoring, incident response, or on-call experience — essential responsibilities for this role despite AI infrastructure background

AI/ML experience appears limited to current role with minimal documented depth; unclear whether candidate has hands-on experience reviewing and extending AI-generated code at the baseline level expected
